title: lactuca viminea subsp. ramosissima
intro: Lactuca viminea subsp. ramosissima, also known as the branching wild lettuce, is a subspecies of the wild lettuce plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant native to Europe, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, which contain lactucarium, a milky sap that acts as a sedative and antispasmodic. The health benefits of Lactuca viminea subsp. ramosissima include relief from insomnia, anxiety, and menstrual cramps, as well as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or tincture for medicinal use.
